description: CLI tool that generates chapter markers for audio files using Gemini multimodal API

userStories.title: CLI argument parsing and validation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run audiochap <input> [options] and get clear errors if arguments are invalid.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI accepts: audiochap <input> [--format json|vtt|srt] [--duration N] [--output FILE]
<input> is required, must be a valid audio file path
--format defaults to json
--duration sets target chapter length in minutes (default: 10)
--output defaults to stdout
Invalid arguments produce clear stderr messages and exit code 1
--help shows usage
Validates GEMINI_API_KEY env var exists at startup, fails immediately if missing

userStories.title: Upload audio to Gemini via File API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to upload audio files to Gemini's File API (always, regardless of size) and get a file URI for use in generation requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Always uses Gemini File API for uploads (no inline audio)
Supports common audio formats (mp3, wav, m4a, ogg, flac)
Returns a file URI usable in subsequent Gemini API calls
Fails fast with clear error on upload failure (network, auth, invalid file)
Handles files up to 100MB (30-60 min audio)

userStories.title: Generate chapters via single Gemini API call
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool to send my audio to Gemini and get chapter markers back in one step.
acceptanceCriteria: Sends uploaded file URI to Gemini multimodal model
Prompt instructs Gemini to generate chapters with timestamps and factual summary titles
Includes --duration value in prompt to control chapter granularity
Forces chapter breaks at target duration boundaries (hard time boundaries)
Parses Gemini response into structured chapter data (timestamp + title)
Fails fast on API errors (rate limit, network, malformed response)

userStories.title: JSON output format
userStories.description: As a user, I want chapter data output as structured JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Default output format
Each chapter has: timestamp (seconds) and title (factual summary)
Output is valid JSON array
Writes to stdout by default, or to --output file path
Silent operation - no progress/status to stdout (errors to stderr only)

userStories.title: VTT output format (chapter navigation cues)
userStories.description: As a user, I want chapter data as a WebVTT chapter file for browser navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Activated via --format vtt
Outputs valid WebVTT with chapter cues (not subtitle cues)
Each cue has start time, end time (= next chapter start), and chapter title
Last chapter ends at audio duration or a reasonable estimate
Conforms to WebVTT spec for chapter navigation

userStories.title: SRT output format
userStories.description: As a user, I want chapter data as an SRT file.
acceptanceCriteria: Activated via --format srt
Outputs valid SRT with sequential numbered entries
Each entry has start timestamp, end timestamp, and chapter title text
Timestamps in HH:MM:SS,mmm format per SRT spec
